data.title: Whitecap Resources, Veren C$15B Merger Deal Bolsters Tariff Resilience — Update
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Canadian oil-and-gas companies Whitecap Resources and Veren are combining in a $10.44 billion deal, expanding their production footprint at a time when energy companies face challenges from tariff threats and volatile commodity prices.

Whitecap Chief Executive Grant Fagerheim said the combined company would be better positioned to navigate both threats, as well as the current macroeconomic backdrop.

The deal, which the companies valued at 15 billion Canadian dollars, including debt, came after President Trump ordered sweeping tariffs on Canadian imports, including a 10% levy on all energy entering the U.S. from its northern neighbor. The tariffs were largely postponed but uncertainty still lingers over whether he will apply them in April and in what form.

Under terms of the deal, Veren shareholders will receive 1.05 common shares of Whitecap for each Veren share owned.

Merging the business and production is expected to reduce costs by about C$200 million annually and provide an immediate 26% boost to Whitecap's free cash flow generation.

Investor reaction was mixed. Whitecap Resources shares fell by more than 12% to C$8.20 in late-morning trading, while Veren's stock jumped 17% to C$8.27.

The combined company will become the largest landholder in the Alberta Montney and the second-largest landholder across unconventional Montney and Duvernay fairways with 1.5 million acres in Alberta. Together, production is expected to reach 370,000 barrels of oil equivalent a day, 63% of which are liquids.

Executives project a stronger financial position post-merger, with lower debt and an improved credit profile anticipated, providing more flexibility for shareholder returns and for long-term capital-structure improvements.

The deal is set to close before May 30.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Moody's Upgrades Viridien on Debt Reduction, Improved Maturity Profile
data.description:
Moody's on Monday upgraded Viridien's long-term corporate family rating to B2 from B3, mainly driven by the France-based geoscience company's lower debt levels and improved maturity profile.

The rating agency said the upgrade also reflects Viridien's good liquidity, its commitment to deleveraging and Moody's expectation of an improvement in the seismic data market.

The outlook on the company was maintained at stable

data.title: Oakbridge adds Higginbotham’s Ledyard as COO
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Private equity-backed Southeast U.S. insurance agency Oakbridge Insurance has hired John Ledyard as executive vice president and COO.

Ledyard will focus on optimising organisational structure, enhancing client services and supporting Oakbridge’s growth.

Oakbridge president and CEO Robbie Smith said: “As we continue to expand our business through both organic and inorganic growth, his leadership will ensure we deliver exceptional value to our clients and insurance carrier partners.”

Ledyard spent the past 10 years at Higginbotham, most recently as managing director, operations.

Alpharetta, Georgia-based Oakbridge was founded in December 2020. It has completed more than 40 acquisitions across the Southeast.

In 2023, middle market-focused Audax Private Equity bought into Oakbridge in a transaction that saw cornerstone backer Corsair Capital divest its stake in the broker.

data.title: RWE Cuts 73 Jobs at U.S. Offshore Wind Division
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

German utility company RWE is cutting 73 jobs in its U.S. offshore wind division in Massachusetts.

Last year, RWE delayed investments in its U.S. offshore wind projects due to "market conditions and increased risk profile."

U.S. President Trump has halted new leases for offshore wind projects and has been critical of the sector.

RWE said in a statement Monday that it cut the roles due to the current regulatory and political environment.

RWE employs around 1,500 people in the U.S.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Adobe GenAI Uncertainty Weighs on Q1, Summit Could Be a Catalyst, Morgan Stanley Says
data.description:
Adobe underperformed its peers due to concerns around its generative AI positioning and growing competition, which has created a low bar for Q1 results, however, the Adobe Summit may provide a positive catalyst, Morgan Stanley said in an earnings preview on Monday.

The company has seen some weakness at the low end of the market, which might be offsetting strength at the higher end. Additionally, the lack of quarterly guidance for digital media net new annual recurring revenue limits investor visibility into the core business, the firm noted.

Morgan Stanley said that the company is preparing for stronger GenAI contributions with new products like Firefly and GenStudio but with potentially longer sales cycles the launches will not impact Q1 results significantly.

"Growth in Digital Media in Q1 was consistent with trends in Q4, and partners pointed to steady seat growth in Creative Cloud despite investor concerns that productivity gains from GenAI tools could reduce seat licenses," the firm said.

Morgan Stanley has an overweight rating on the company's stock with a price target of $660.

data.title: Microsoft's Azure Platform Faces Strong Medium-Term Tailwinds, UBS Says
data.description:
Microsoft shares have weakened since its Q2 earnings report, partly due to "unexpected" slower growth in its Azure cloud computing platform, and the uncertainty about the cause and timeline for improvement is affecting investor confidence, UBS said in a note Sunday.

The analysts said the company has cited mid-2024 changes in its go-to-market, or GTM, strategy, but ambiguity about the root cause and the duration of the fix is affecting the stock. The analysts added that after speaking with several Microsoft partners, they found that changes to licensing, channel incentives, and account management in mid-2024 likely disrupted Azure sales, and it may take several quarters to resolve.

In addition to the GTM changes, other macro factors have also contributed to the slowdown, such as slower large-scale migration activity and some customers under-spending relative to their commitments. These broader issues are reflected in the results of Microsoft's competitors, such as Amazon's AWS and Alphabet's Google Cloud, the analysts said. They added that despite these challenges, if Azure can still achieve 31% total growth and 18% growth excluding AI, it suggests that the underlying growth drivers for Azure remain solid. However, with investor expectations for Q4 Azure growth set at 32-33%, the analysts believe these projections may be too high.

"Investor sentiment is decidedly mixed, based primarily on concerns about what exactly is weighing on core Azure growth and how long the fix might take," the analysts said, adding that they believe medium-term tailwinds "remain strong" on Azure but that investors "can afford to remain patient" in the near term.

UBS has a buy rating and a $510 price target on Microsoft.

data.title: European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Fall in Monday Trading
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were falling late Monday morning, dropping 2.31% on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index to 1,411.83.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical company DBV Technologies and petroleum refiner Equinor , which rose 6.4% and 5.1%, respectively. They were followed by medical device maker EDAP TMS and consumer goods giant Unilever , which were up 4.8% and 3.1%, respectively.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk and financial services company Banco Santander , which fell 10% and 5.7%, respectively. They were followed by biotech firm Evaxion Biotech and internet browser company Opera , which were down 5.3% each.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical company Biodexa Pharmaceuticals and utilities company National Grid , which advanced 11% and 2.8%, respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical company TC Biopharm and oil and gas company BP , which increased 2.3% and 1.4%, respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by biopharmaceutical companies Verona Pharma and Akari Therapeutics , which lost 7.5% and 7.3%, respectively. They were followed by cruise line operator Carnival and financial services company Barclays , which shed 7.2% each.

data.title: DraftKings' Live Betting Volumes Drive Growth Investments — Market Talk
data.description: Around half of DraftKings' total volume comes from live betting and the sports wagering company is aiming to ramp up investments in that area, Benchmark analyst Mike Hickey says. The company is integrating low-latency streaming into its app to address delays in broadcasting. DraftKings has also acquired two technology companies, Simplebet and Sports IQ Analytics, to maintain and grow revenue from live betting, the company said during an investor event with Morgan Stanley last week. In February, DraftKings raised the low end and midpoint of its revenue range for 2025 due to investments in live betting, which it expects to be Ebitda positive starting in 2026. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Monday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Whitecap Resources, Veren to Merge in a $10.4 Billion All-Share Deal

Whitecap Resources and Veren have agreed to combine their companies in $10.44 billion deal that will enlarge their Alberta oil and gas footprint.

Rocket to Buy Redfin in $1.75 Billion All-Stock Deal

The mortgage giant Rocket Cos. has agreed to buy Redfin in an all-stock deal valuing the online real-estate brokerage at $1.75 billion.

Novo Nordisk Shares Fall After Latest CagriSema Trial Results

New trial data showed the drug helped obese or overweight type 2 diabetics lose an average 15.7% of their weight, when an earlier study recorded 22.7%.

Beacon Roofing Supply, QXO Discussing Potential Deal

Beacon Roofing Supply and QXO have launched discussions about a potential combination in which Brad Jacobs' company would acquire Beacon for around $7.7 billion.

7-Eleven Owner, Couche-Tard Financial Advisers Looking for Potential Buyers for Some Stores

Seven & i Holdings said in a letter to shareholders Monday that Couche-Tard recently agreed to jointly explore a potential sale of some stores prior to signing a takeover agreement.

Foxconn Builds FoxBrain, Its Own AI Model

The world's largest contract electronics maker said that aided by Nvidia it had built a large language model with reasoning capabilities, developed in-house and trained in four weeks.

Report Supports Case for Rio Tinto Unification in Australia, Palliser Says

The activist investor wants Rio Tinto to abandon its primary London listing and unify its corporate structure into a single Australian-domiciled company.

Investors Want a Piece of DeepSeek. Its Founder Says Not Now.

The chatbot startup has problems, but its founder doesn't want new shareholders to be one of them.

Logistics Startups Are Back in Demand as Companies Navigate Tariffs

Prominent during the pandemic, the startups are now offering software to help companies scenario-plan for a bundle of new tariffs; 'There's definitely a ton of demand,' one CEO says.

CVS's New Mini Stores Go All In on Medicine and Skip Everything Else

The national drugstore chain is preparing to open a dozen stores offering full-service pharmacies but very limited retail.
